ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT JUAN VELASQUEZ'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER OF A SEPARATE TRIAL AND TO TRANSFER ACTION AND MONETARY SANCTIONS
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-13-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JUAN VELASQUEZ' MOTION FOR AN ORDER OF A SEPARATE TRIAL AND TO TRANSFER ACTION AND MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. IT IS PREMATURE TO DETERMINE THAT SEPARATE TRIALS WILL NECESSARILY BE APPROPRIATE IF THE TWO DEFENDANTS REMAIN IN THIS CASE. CCP SEC. 1048 ON WHICH THIS MOTION IS PREMISED PERMITS SEPARATE TRIALS, BUT IS NOT A PROPER MECHANISM TO RAISE MISJOINDER OF PARTIES. THEREFORE, THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE WHETHER VENUE WOULD BE PROPER IF MISJOINDER WERE DETERMINED. THIS DECISION IS, HOWEVER, WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RAISING MISJOINDER OF THE DEFENDANTS PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 430.10(D), IF THAT REMAINS AN AVAILABLE OPTION, OR IN SOME OTHER PROCEDURALLY PROPER WAY. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185
ANSWER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT VELASQUEZ, JUAN

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for a Hearing on a Minor's Compromise.
Department 514, Judge: Charles Crompton, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Mitch Lyon, #6840.
Nicholas J. Peterson, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff(s) Petitioner: Suhila Gebrail, mother and guardian ad litem for Minor: Raya Gebrail; present in court.
Petitioner sworn and examinded. Spanish Interpreter: sworn.
After testimony and consideration of the evidence presented, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved as follows:
Total Settlement: $16,100.00
Attorney's Fees: $3,768.00 payable to Koarnblum, Cochran, Erickson, & Harbison, LLP
Medical, hospital, ambulance, nursing, and other like expenses payable diretly to providers as follows, in the total amount of: $3,848.00
UCFW & Employers Benefit Trust 2200 Professional Dr., Ste. 200, Roseville, CA 95661
Other authorized disbursements payable directly to third parties in the total amount of $1,028.00 to Kornblum, Cochran, Erickson & Harbison for costs.
Total allowance for fees and expenses from the settlement of judgment: $8,644.00
A net balance of $7,456.00 to be deposited into a blocked account at Chase Bank, 5655 Geary Boulevard, San Francisco, CA 94121 until Minor reaches the age of 18 years.
Court signed Order Granting approval of Minor and Order to Deposit Money into blocked Account.
Status Hearing Scheduled on April 28, 2015 at 9:00 AM, in Department 514 for filing of receipt for the deposit of money into blocked account. No appearance is required if the receipt is filed and courtesy copy lodge with Depart. 514 prior to Hearing.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Of A Separate Trial And To Transfer Action And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-13-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: NICHOLAS PETERSON, ESQ., 415.440.7800 X220, FOR PLAINTIFFS SUHILA GEBRAIL ET AL; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JUAN VELASQUEZ' Motion For An Order Of A Separate Trial And To Transfer Action And Monetary Sanctions IS Denied without prejudice. It is premature to determine that separate trials will necessarily be appropriate if the two defendants remain in this case. CCP sec. 1048 on which this motion is premised permits separate trials, but is not a proper mechanism to raise misjoinder of parties. Therefore, the Court cannot determine whether venue would be proper if misjoinder were determined. This decision is, however, without prejudice to raising misjoinder of the defendants pursuant to CCP sec. 430.10(d), if that remains an available option, or in some other procedurally proper way. All requests for sanctions are denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185
